part3Update: The fix validated here with isolated fixtures is now exercised for real: TEN_K__4D__INTERMEDIATE v1/v2/v3 were retired via the real retirement ledger, and 0.6.0-pilot contains exactly one TEN_K__4D__INTERMEDIATE bundle (v4). See retirement-ledger-application.json and zero-six-pilot-release-audit.json.
question: Does CatalogPublisher's full-catalog packaging path exclude a RETIRED combination itself (not just a retired dependency) from new releases?
findingBeforeFix: NO — confirmed defect. CatalogPublisher.BuildRelease iterated every combination in the full catalog source snapshot with no eligibility filter; CatalogBundleAssembler.Assemble only checked retirement of a combination's 8 dependency objects, never the combination's own Metadata.

callPathTable.behaviorForRetiredCombinationBeforeFix: Loaded unconditionally — correct by design, this layer must not filter.
DEFECT: every combination got a bundle assembled and included in the new release regardless of its own retirement status.
Did not block a retired combination itself.
Republished the retired combination's source file and bundle into the new release, indistinguishable from an active one.
Would silently succeed for an explicitly-requested retired combination.

files: src/PlanCatalog.Infrastructure/Publishing/CatalogBundleAssembler.cs — added combination.Metadata retirement check before dependency resolution; throws InvalidOperationException prefixed RETIRED_COMBINATION_NOT_ELIGIBLE_FOR_NEW_RELEASE

src/PlanCatalog.Infrastructure/Publishing/CatalogPublisher.cs — filters stamped.Combinations into eligibleCombinations (non-retired) before bundle assembly, manifest artifact listing, and release file packaging via stampedForRelease = stamped with { Combinations = eligibleCombinations }
fixApplied.mechanismReused: IRetirementLedger (existing port, no duplicate mechanism introduced)
fixApplied.schemaAndGraphValidationScope: unchanged — still runs against the full unfiltered snapshot for audit/correctness purposes

testsAdded.isolation: in-memory ICatalogSourceRepository fake built from CombinationFixture plus a second independently-keyed combination (RETIREMENT_TEST_COMBINATION); never touches real catalog/ or real retirements.json

regressionVerification.build: dotnet build -c Release — succeeded, 0 warnings, 0 errors
regressionVerification.realCatalogFullBuildPreview: build-release preview against the real catalog/ tree still produces exactly 2 PUBLISHED_TEMPLATE_BUNDLE artifacts (TEN_K__4D__INTERMEDIATE v1 and v2) — unchanged, because artifacts/appsel-plan-catalog/retirements.json does not exist and nothing is currently retired in real data. Confirms the fix is a true no-op for current production data.

invariantsEnforced: A retired combination remains in source/historical releases for audit — unaffected, no source deletion or discovery-level filtering
A retired combination may still be used to verify historical releases — proven by HistoricalRelease_BuiltBeforeRetirement_StillVerifiesAfterCombinationIsLaterRetired
A retired combination must NOT produce a bundle in any newly built release — enforced by CatalogPublisher.BuildRelease's eligibleCombinations filter
A retired combination must not appear as an active/newly-published root combination — excluded from new release's Artifacts and Bundles
A retired combination must not be selected by default — full-catalog packaging silently skips it
Explicit requests for a retired combination fail clearly — RETIRED_COMBINATION_NOT_ELIGIBLE_FOR_NEW_RELEASE from CatalogBundleAssembler.Assemble
No duplicate retirement mechanism introduced — reuses existing IRetirementLedger exclusively
